title: length (deprecated)
text: length (deprecated) Return the number of characters in the specified string. Note: The length statement is deprecated. It has been superseded by $length function, which is applicable to all component types. length String Example: length vString Parameters String —string, or a field (or indirect reference to a field), a variable, or a function that evaluates to a string. Return Values $result is set to the number of characters in the string. $status is not affected. Use Allowed in all component types. Description The length statement finds the length of the piece of text in String . The value of $result is set to the number of characters in String . If String contains frame marker, ruler, or character attribute characters, they are ignored. The length statement is often used to determine where extraction should begin. Example: Find the Length of a String The following example finds the length of the string in vString: ; store contents of NOTEPAD in vString ; how long is string in vString? vString = "test string" length vString vLength = $result OUTPUT = vLength Copy Example: Finding the Offset to Characters in a String The following example uses the length statement to find the offset to the last two characters in the string. In the example, the field INVNUM contains the value 90021387SH. This is the concatenation of a date (13-Feb-1990), a number (87), and a salesman's initials (SH): ; how long is invnum? ; $1 contains position second from end ; extract last 2 characters ; report their total sales length INVNUM $1=$result-1 $2=INVNUM[$1] selectdb (sum(AMOUNT)) %/ from "INVOICES" %/ u_where (SALESCODE = $2) %/ to SALESREPORT.DUMMY Copy Related Links $length Return the length of the specified argument. $syntax Convert a string to a syntax string. $result Return the result of certain ProcScript commands and tree widget events.
